newsSinceJun6: Jun 8: record fiscal Q3 FY26 results (net sales +9% YoY to $126.3M, net income +25%, gross margin 26.7% vs 28.6%, SG&A down to $16.6M), driven mainly by elevated cat litter demand across B2B and Retail/Wholesale; earnings call Jun 9 10:00 CT. Also Jun 3: 23rd consecutive annual dividend increase (~10%, to $0.225/sh common) plus 500K-share buyback authorization. Stock +15.4% today on the print - move is fundamental, but the upside driver is consumer/ag sorbents, not the AI-facility pathway.

sources.label: GlobeNewswire - Oil-Dri Reports Record Third Quarter Revenues and Strong Earnings Growth (Jun 8, 2026)
GlobeNewswire - Oil-Dri Increases Dividends for 23rd Consecutive Year and Authorizes Stock Repurchases (Jun 3, 2026)
SEC 8-K exhibit - ODC FY26 Q3 earnings release

verdicts.rankRationale: The +15.4% earnings pop (record Q3, dividend hike, buyback) is real fundamental news, but it is entirely cat-litter/consumer driven and adds zero evidence for the latent AI-facility sorbents pathway this page screens for. At +100% YTD and a fresh rerating on a non-AI catalyst, the name is no longer hidden or under-owned; residual alpha on this lens has compressed.
verdicts.bull: A well-run, vertically integrated clay minerals monopoly-adjacent producer with 25% net income growth, pricing power, and untapped optionality in fluid purification and facility sorbents if data-center filtration/spill demand ever materializes.
verdicts.neutral: Strong execution and shareholder returns justify the rerating on quality grounds, but the AI-node link remains speculative and unproven; the stock is now priced as a discovered compounder rather than a latent node.
verdicts.bear: Gross margin compressed (26.7% vs 28.6%) on input-cost inflation, the AI pathway has produced no contracts or named wins, and a $1.4B small-cap that doubled in six months on pet-litter demand is vulnerable to multiple mean-reversion once momentum buyers rotate.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any concrete disclosure of fluid-purification or industrial-sorbent design wins tied to data-center or AI-facility customers (next checkpoint: FY26 Q4 results, ~September 2026).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating chase: paying up after a +15% day and +100% YTD for a thesis whose AI-relevance is still entirely latent; margin pressure from cost inflation.
